title: vatica granulata
intro: Vatica granulata, also known as the Borneo ironwood, is a species of tree native to the island of Borneo in Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high tannin content. The health benefits of Vatica granulata include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In traditional herbal preparations, the bark and leaves of the tree are often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
